set.caption: Figure 3.8: Computing FOLLOW Sets for Non-Terminal Symbols. for each $A \in NT$ do; FOLLOW(A) ← ∅; end; FOLLOW(S) ← {eof}; while (FOLLOW sets are still changing) do; for each $p \in P$ of the form $A \to \beta_{1}\beta_{2} \cdots \beta_{k}$ do; TRAILER ← FOLLOW(A); for i ← k down to 1 do; if $\beta_{i} \in NT$ then begin; FOLLOW( $\beta_{i}$ ) ← FOLLOW( $\beta_{i}$ ) ∪ TRAILER; if $\epsilon \in \text{FIRST}(\beta_{i})$ then TRAILER ← TRAILER ∪ (FIRST( $\beta_{i}$ ) - $\epsilon$ ); else TRAILER ← FIRST( $\beta_{i}$ ); end; else TRAILER ← FIRST( $\beta_{i}$ ); // is $\{\beta_{i}\}$ end; end; end;

set.caption: Figure 4.3: Schema for Implementing Addition with Runtime Type Checking. ```txt // partial code for "a+b ⇒ c" if (tag(a) = integer) then if (tag(b) = integer) then value(c) = value(a) + value(b); tag(c) = integer; else if (tag(b) = real) then temp = ConvertToReal(a); value(c) = temp + value(b); tag(c) = real; else if (tag(b) = ...) then // handle all other types ... else signal runtime type fault else if (tag(a) = real) then if (tag(b) = integer) then temp = ConvertToReal(b); value(c) = value(a) + temp; tag(c) = real; else if (tag(b) = real) then value(c) = value(a) + value(b); tag(c) = real; else if (tag(b) = ...) then // handle all other types ... else signal runtime type fault else if (tag(a) = ...) then // handle all other types ... else signal illegal tag value; ```

overrides.reason: Block is the in-book Figure 4.3 caption/code/table but was excluded or typed without semantic figure id.

set.caption: Figure 4.14: Building an Abstract Syntax Tree and Inferring Expression Types. ```txt Production Syntax-Directed Actions Expr → Expr + Term { $$ ← MakeNode2 (plus, $1, $3); $$.type ← F+(1.type, $3.type) } | Expr - Term { $$ ← MakeNode2 (minus, $1, $3); $$.type ← F-(1.type, $3.type) } | Term { $$ ← $1 } Term → Term × Factor { $$ ← MakeNode2 (times, $1, $3); $$.type ← F×(1.type, $3.type) } | Term ÷ Factor { $$ ← MakeNode2 (divide, $1, $3); $$.type ← F÷(1.type, $3.type) } | Factor { $$ ← $1 } Factor → (Expr) { $$ ← $2 } | num { $$ ← MakeNode0(number); $$.text ← scanned text; $$.type ← type of the number } | name { $$ ← MakeNode0(identifier); $$.text ← scanned text; $$.type ← type of the identifier } ```
